title: ERC4337StaticSigDoS CONSTANTS
description: TLC model constants for the ERC4337StaticSigDoS bug-class shape. The spec models a wallet whose static-signature check is keyed on msg.sender, and a call path that arrives via the EntryPoint (so msg.sender = EntryPoint, not the expected User-signer). The bug fires when EntryPoint != User AND when the call path is ViaEntryPoint (controlled by PathChoice).

Calls.description: Set of call identities. Use 1-3 calls. Each call is a short identity token like c1, c2, c3.

EntryPoint.description: Identity of the ERC-4337 EntryPoint contract. Short lowercase token like 'ep'. MUST DIFFER from User for the bug to manifest.

User.description: Identity of the user-EOA / expected signer. Short lowercase token like 'u'. MUST DIFFER from EntryPoint for the bug to manifest.

PathChoice.description: Which call path to model: 'Direct' (EOA → wallet, msg.sender=User) or 'ViaEntryPoint' (EOA → EntryPoint → wallet, msg.sender=EntryPoint). LEAD-CONDITIONED: if the audit lead mentions EntryPoint, ERC-4337, 4337, validateUserOp, UserOperation, msg.sender forwarding in a 4337 context, bundler, or account abstraction with EntryPoint, set PathChoice='ViaEntryPoint'. Otherwise (unrelated bug class, e.g., reentrancy, overflow, replay without 4337 context) set PathChoice='Direct'. This gates the buggy action: only ViaEntryPoint fires the invariant violation.
